items.question: ___ earth goes around ___ sun.
explanations: Correct! Unique things take 'the': 'The earth goes around the sun'.
'A/a' is for non-specific, non-unique things.
Unique things need 'the', not zero article.
The sun is unique, so it takes 'the', not 'a'.

items.question: I bought a jacket. ___ jacket was too small.
explanations: Correct! Second mention takes 'the': 'The jacket was too small'.
'A jacket' would introduce a new one.
'An' is for a vowel sound and would mean a new one.
The jacket is now known, so it needs 'the'.

items.question: Can you close ___ window, please?
explanations: Correct! Both people know which window: 'the window'.
'A window' would suggest any window, which is odd here.
'An' is for a vowel sound.
A specific, known window needs 'the'.

items.question: She's ___ best student in the class.
explanations: Correct! Superlatives take 'the': 'the best student'.
Superlatives don't take 'a'.
Superlatives don't take 'an'.
A superlative needs 'the'.

items.question: They sailed down ___ Nile.
explanations: Correct! Rivers take 'the': 'the Nile'.
Rivers don't take 'a'.
Rivers take 'the', not zero article.
Rivers don't take 'an'.

items.question: My brother lives in ___ United States.
explanations: Correct! Plural/'united' country names take 'the': 'the United States'.
Countries don't take 'a'.
This name takes 'the', unlike France or Japan.
Countries don't take 'an'.

items.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: The dogs are loyal animals in general.
explanations: For animals in general, don't use 'the'.
Correct! General statements about a whole group take no article: 'Dogs are loyal'.
'A' can't go with a plural noun.
'The dog are' mixes singular and plural.

items.question: It was ___ first time I had seen snow.
explanations: Correct! Ordinals take 'the': 'the first time'.
Ordinals don't take 'a' here.
Ordinals don't take 'an'.
'First' needs 'the'.

items.question: Correct this: 'Sun rises in the east.'
options: The sun rises in the east.
A sun rises in the east.
The sun rises in a east.
explanations: Correct! Unique things take 'the': 'The sun rises in the east'.
The sun is unique, so it takes 'the', not 'a'.
'The east' is a specific direction; 'an east' is wrong.
'A east' is wrong — use 'the east'.

items.question: When do you use 'the'?
options: When the listener knows exactly which one you mean.
When the noun is one of many.
When talking about a group in general.
When the noun is a job title.
explanations: Correct! 'The' signals a specific, known noun.
One of many usually takes a/an.
General statements often take no article.
Jobs take a/an: 'She's a nurse'.
